_comment: Structured research grounding for the 12 constructs. Single source of truth for the research links the visual explorer surfaces. Prose lives in docs/research/*.md; this file holds the citable anchors. Every url here was checked against a real source. No fabricated metrics. Non-clinical: these anchors ground the CONSTRUCT MODELS, never the profiling of a person.
thesis: This System is a non-clinical prediction primitive: it observes cognitive signals and predicts likely responses with confidence, never a diagnosis. That is the human-mind analog of the paradigm frontier labs are building toward in machines — world models and predictive processing, where intelligence is framed as predicting the next state of a system in a learned latent space. The same frame runs through cognitive science (the Bayesian/predictive brain) and through the affective-computing line on predicting emotional response to viewed content. We borrow the framing, not the autonomy: the human owns every decision.
nonClinicalBoundary: The affective-computing research below models emotion ELICITED BY CONTENT/MEDIA and grounds the `emotion` construct's lenses. It is cited to ground the model, not to profile, score, surveil, or diagnose any person. Per CANON.md this boundary is non-waivable. No anchor here licenses emotion-detection-on-people or any clinical reading.

frontier.title: A Path Towards Autonomous Machine Intelligence (JEPA / LV-EBM)
frontier.note: Frames machine intelligence around a world model that predicts future representations in latent space rather than generating raw output. The architectural mirror of this System's observe→predict move.

frontier.title: I-JEPA: self-supervised learning by predicting in representation space
frontier.note: First model on LeCun's vision; predicts abstract latent representations, not pixels. V-JEPA (Bardes et al. 2024) and V-JEPA 2 (Assran et al. 2025) extend the line to video dynamics.

frontier.title: Cosmos World Foundation Model Platform for Physical AI
frontier.note: A world foundation model: a large system trained to understand, simulate, and predict physical environments. The clearest current statement of 'model the world to predict it.'

frontier.title: Understanding World or Predicting Future? A Survey of World Models
frontier.note: Map of the world-model landscape; situates JEPA, Cosmos, and prediction-vs-generation debates.

frontier.title: Predictive coding / the predictive brain
frontier.note: The brain as a prediction engine minimizing prediction error across a cortical hierarchy. The cognitive-science root of the prediction frame.

frontier.title: Bayesian approaches to brain function (active inference, free energy)
frontier.note: Perception and action as Bayesian inference; action selected to minimize expected free energy. Grounds the motivation/behavior/belief edges.

frontier.title: An Introduction to Predictive Processing Models of Perception and Decision-Making
frontier.note: Accessible, current synthesis of predictive processing across perception and decision-making.

frontier.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in Theory of Mind Tasks
frontier.note: False-belief battery on LLMs; reports GPT-4-class performance near older-child levels. Treated as a measurement result with known training-contamination caveats, not proof of mind.

frontier.title: Computational model mimics humans' ability to predict emotions
frontier.note: A model predicting others' emotional reactions from situation appraisal — appraisal theory made computational.

frontier.title: Decoding viewer emotions in video ads
frontier.note: Predicting emotional response ELICITED BY video content. Cited to ground the emotion construct's lenses; not a tool for profiling viewers.

frontier.title: Large Language Model Psychometrics: A Systematic Review
frontier.note: How mental-construct measurement is being applied to models; relevant to identity/metacognition framing, with validity caveats.

attention.frame: Predictive processing recasts attention as precision-weighting — the brain up-weights prediction errors it expects to be informative. JEPA's masked-prediction objective is a machine echo: learn by predicting the masked, salient part.

memory.frame: World models carry a latent state that persists and is updated — a functional memory. The construct's classic encoding/storage/retrieval lenses sit under that frame; durable change still routes through memory.

emotion.frame: The user's named anchor: predicting emotional response to viewed content. Appraisal theory makes this computational (situation → predicted affect); the dimensional valence-arousal lens makes it measurable. Grounds the model, never profiles a person.

motivation.frame: Active inference selects action to minimize expected free energy rather than to chase reward — a prediction-first account of what gets pursued. Maps the emotion→motivation and identity→motivation edges.

identity.frame: The self-model as a stable, narrative prior the system maintains. LLM-psychometrics work probes whether models carry stable trait-like profiles — a cautionary mirror, with validity caveats, for how we model identity.

learning.frame: JEPA and world foundation models learn by minimizing prediction error in latent space — the engineering of 'durable change from experience.' The behavior→learning feedback edge is the loop these systems formalize.

belief.frame: Belief as a prior updated by evidence — the Bayesian brain. Learning modulates belief (Bayesian-style update); social-cognition modulates it too (social inference shifts priors).

behavior.frame: In active inference, action is how a system makes its predictions come true — changing the input to fit the model. Observable behavior is where committed intention and belief become signal the model can read.

consciousness.frame: The most contested construct. Predictive-processing accounts treat reportable (access) content as the substrate metacognition inspects. The System stays strictly descriptive here and makes no phenomenal claims.

metacognition.frame: Monitoring and control of one's own cognition — the self-correction layer. Confidence and calibration are prediction-about-prediction; model-uncertainty work is the engineering parallel.

decision-making.frame: Predictive processing frames choice as expected-free-energy minimization; prospect theory and dual-process models (the schema's lenses) sit inside it. Emotion, belief, motivation, and metacognition all feed this node.

social-cognition.frame: Theory of mind — modeling other minds — is the construct frontier labs probe most directly in models. Kosinski's false-belief battery is a measurement of that capacity (with training-contamination caveats), not proof of mind.
